text: Artificial intelligence is changing many areas of our lives, from hiring to healthcare. However, as AI becomes more common, important ethical questions arise. One issue is bias. If AI learns from data that contains unfair patterns, it may make biased decisions, for example, against certain groups. This is why ensuring fairness is crucial. Another concern is privacy: AI systems often use large amounts of personal data, so users must know how their information is collected and used. Transparency is also key—people should understand how AI reaches its conclusions. To address these challenges, responsible AI development is necessary. This means creating rules and practices that avoid harm and promote trust.
